steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence contains a closing em dash before "thrived," which closes a parenthetical appositive listing specific lions (Rasta, Pretty Boy, and Makhulu). A closing em dash requires a matching opening em dash to bracket the interruption correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "coalition—": places the opening em dash at the start of the appositive, creating the required matched pair: coalition — … Makhulu — thrived. ✓
  • "coalition;": a semicolon cannot pair with a closing em dash; the punctuation marks would be mismatched. ✗
  • "coalition": leaving no opening mark with a closing em dash later is ungrammatical. ✗
  • "coalition,": a comma cannot open a construction closed by an em dash. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
